headline: Ofgem cuts energy price cap to £1,641; ONS reports £14.3bn February borrowing

items.title: Ofgem energy price cap falls to £1,641 for Q2 2026
items.summary: The energy regulator announced a 6.6% cut to the default tariff cap from April, driven by lower wholesale costs and the government's decision to shift some policy costs to general taxation. Electricity unit rates fall from 27.69p to 24.67p/kWh. However, network costs rose £66, partly offsetting the wholesale savings.

items.title: Public sector net borrowing hit £14.3bn in February 2026
items.summary: ONS public finances data shows borrowing of £14.3bn in February, with national debt at 93.1% of GDP. Debt interest costs continue to track above £111bn annually. The fiscal position remains constrained by low growth and high servicing costs.

items.title: Government announces £50m+ heating oil support package
items.summary: New support for off-grid households using heating oil, alongside signals of further energy regulation changes. The move addresses rural energy poverty but doesn't tackle the structural electricity pricing issues.

items.title: ONS reports retail sales and inflation data
items.summary: Latest ONS releases show continued pressure on consumer spending amid persistent inflation in services. Real wage growth remains minimal despite headline employment figures.
